title: Eldorado Mountains

infobox.value: [Ireteba Peak](/w/index.php?title=Ireteba_Peak&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ireteba Peak (page does not exist)")[[1]](#cite_note-1), Eldorado Mountains

of 320,060 acres (129,520 ha). Established in 2004, the area is jointly managed by the [NPS](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") and the [BLM](/wiki/Bureau_of_Land_Management "Bureau of Land Management"). The NPS manages the eastern section within the [Lake Mead National Recreation Area](/wiki/Lake_Mead_National_Recreation_Area "Lake Mead National Recreation Area") and the BLM manages the western section.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mountain range in Nevada, US

Nevada is at right, and the prominence, forward. Arizona's ([Black Mountains (Arizona)](/wiki/Black_Mountains_(Arizona) "Black Mountains (Arizona)") is on left bank. The view is downstream & slightly west-southwestwards. | |
| Highest point | |
| Peak | [Ireteba Peak](/w/index.php?title=Ireteba_Peak&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ireteba Peak (page does not exist)")[[1]](#cite_note-1), Eldorado Mountains |

| [Range coordinates](/wiki/Geographic_coordinate_system "Geographic coordinate system") | [35°48′26.944″N 114°46′57.927″W﻿ / ﻿35.80748444°N 114.78275750°W﻿ / 35.80748444; -114.78275750](https://geohack.toolforge.org/geohack.php?pagename=Eldorado_Mountains&params=35_48_26.944_N_114_46_57.927_W_type:mountain_scale:300000) |
| [Topo map](/wiki/Topographic_map "Topographic map") | [USGS](/wiki/USGS "USGS") Boulder City SE |

The **Eldorado Mountains**, also called the **El Dorado Mountains**, are a north-south trending mountain range in southeast [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada") bordering west of the south-flowing [Colorado River](/wiki/Colorado_River "Colorado River"); the [endorheic](/wiki/Endorheic "Endorheic") [Eldorado Valley](/wiki/Eldorado_Valley "Eldorado Valley") borders the range to the west, and the range is also on the western border of the Colorado River's [Black Canyon of the Colorado](/wiki/Black_Canyon_of_the_Colorado "Black Canyon of the Colorado"), and [El Dorado Canyon](/wiki/El_Dorado_Canyon_(Nevada) "El Dorado Canyon (Nevada)") on the river. The range is 50 miles (80 km) southeast of [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas,_Nevada "Las Vegas, Nevada"), Nevada; and the Eldorado Mountains connect with the [Highland](/wiki/Highland_Mountains_(Nevada) "Highland Mountains (Nevada)") and [Newberry](/wiki/Newberry_Mountains_(Nevada) "Newberry Mountains (Nevada)") mountains.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

The name *El Dorado* is taken from the name of a legendary [gold](/wiki/Gold "Gold") mine, and the ghost town-mining location of [Nelson](/wiki/Nelson,_Nevada "Nelson, Nevada") in Nelson Canyon, transects the central portion of the range. The *Nelson Overlook* is a viewing point over the Colorado River's El Dorado Canyon.

## Wilderness

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Eldorado_Mountains&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Wilderness")]

The mountain range contains the **Eldorado Wilderness** of 320,060 acres (129,520 ha). Established in 2004, the area is jointly managed by the [NPS](/wiki/National_Park_Service "National Park Service") and the [BLM](/wiki/Bureau_of_Land_Management "Bureau of Land Management"). The NPS manages the eastern section within the [Lake Mead National Recreation Area](/wiki/Lake_Mead_National_Recreation_Area "Lake Mead National Recreation Area") and the BLM manages the western section.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

This desert wilderness is a rugged maze of hills, peaks, and winding canyons. The Eldorado Range is [volcanic rock](/wiki/Volcanic_rock "Volcanic rock") with [basalt](/wiki/Basalt "Basalt") flows on a base of [metamorphic rock](/wiki/Metamorphic_rock "Metamorphic rock"). A [bajada](/wiki/Bajada_(geography) "Bajada (geography)") extends northwest of the mountains. [Creosote bush](/wiki/Creosote_bush "Creosote bush"), [scrub oak](/wiki/Scrub_oak "Scrub oak"), clump grass, and various cacti cover the mountains and foothills. This supports a significant population of [bighorn sheep](/wiki/Bighorn_sheep "Bighorn sheep") in the higher elevations. Abandoned mine sites are scattered among the region. Water is scarce, besides the Colorado River, and summer temperatures can reach 120 °F (48 °C).[[5]](#cite_note-5)

To the south, [Ireteba Peaks Wilderness](/wiki/Ireteba_Peaks_Wilderness "Ireteba Peaks Wilderness") of 32,745 acres (13,251 ha) is composed of the *Ireteba Ridge* at 5,013 feet (1,528 m),[[6]](#cite_note-6) that overlooks to the east, the northern portions of [Lake Mohave](/wiki/Lake_Mohave "Lake Mohave") and the El Dorado Canyon. The high point of the Eldorado Mountains is Ireteba Peak at 5,060 feet (1,542 m), which is named for the [Mohave](/wiki/Mohave_people "Mohave people") Indian guide and tribal leader [Irataba](/wiki/Irataba "Irataba").

Some communities associated with the range, are in the south, [Searchlight](/wiki/Searchlight,_Nevada "Searchlight, Nevada") on [US 95](/wiki/U.S._Route_95_in_Nevada "U.S. Route 95 in Nevada") and [Cottonwood Cove](/wiki/Cottonwood_Cove,_Nevada "Cottonwood Cove, Nevada"), on the river. Nelson is in the center of the range, and the nearest community north is [Boulder City](/wiki/Boulder_City,_Nevada "Boulder City, Nevada").
